---
title: "Where OrgTree & OrgPath Data Lives"
subtitle: "Codebook in Git, Values in Entra ID, Authority in HR"
date: 2026-05-11
---

::: {.callout-note}
## A clarifying explainer

A recurring question from operators reading the OrgTree canon for the first
time is "where is this data actually stored?" — is it in a registry checked
into the repo, in Entra ID extension attributes, in HR, or somewhere else?
This page answers that question in one place. The authoritative specs remain
[UIAO_151 (Codebook)](codebook.qmd) and
[UIAO_158 (JSON Schema)](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_158_OrgPath_JSON_Schema.md).
:::

## The short answer

There is no single "OrgPath registry." OrgPath data lives in **three places
at once**, each with a distinct role:

| Layer | Where it lives | What it holds | Role |
|-------|---------------|---------------|------|
| **Vocabulary** | `src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml` (Git) | The enumerated set of *valid* OrgPath codes, hierarchy, regex, lifecycle status | Defines the language |
| **Assignment** | `extensionAttribute1` on each user / device in **Entra ID** | The *actual* OrgPath value carried by every principal | Carries the runtime data |
| **Authority** | The **HR system** (Workday, SAP HR, agency HRIS, etc.) | The ground-truth org structure that drives values | Source of truth |

If the three disagree, the resolution order is **HR → Entra → Codebook
validation → Drift event**. IT never edits `extensionAttribute1` by hand.

## Layer 1 — The vocabulary lives in Git

The Codebook is a YAML file checked into the monorepo. It is not a database
and not a registry service — it is a versioned, PR-reviewed document.

- **File:** [`src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml)
- **Schema:** [`src/uiao/schemas/orgpath/codebook.schema.json`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/schemas/orgpath/codebook.schema.json)
- **Canon doc:** [`UIAO_151_OrgPath_Codebook.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_151_OrgPath_Codebook.md)
- **JSON Schema spec:** [`UIAO_158_OrgPath_JSON_Schema.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_158_OrgPath_JSON_Schema.md)

Each entry in the codebook is an `OrgPathEntry` with:

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `code` | string | Must match `^ORG(-[A-Z0-9]{2,6}){0,8}$` |
| `level` | int 0–8 | Root = 0, Division = 1, … Squad = 8 |
| `description` | string | Human-readable label |
| `parentPath` | string \| null | Parent code (null at root) |
| `status` | enum | `active` · `deprecated` · `pending` |
| `owner` | string | Responsible role |
| `createdDate` · `modifiedDate` | ISO 8601 | Audit trail |

Codebook changes follow the canonical Contributor Workflow
([UIAO_172](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_172_Canonical_Contributor_Workflow.md))
— PR, CI validation, review, merge, provenance chain.

## Layer 2 — The values live in Entra ID

The actual OrgPath assigned to a given person or device is stored on the
Entra ID directory object itself, in a Microsoft-provided extension
attribute slot:

| Principal type | Storage location |
|----------------|-----------------|
| **User** | `user.extensionAttribute1` |
| **Device (Entra-joined)** | `device.extensionAttribute1` |
| **Azure resource (ARM)** | ARM tag named `OrgPath` |
| **Service principal** | `extensionAttribute1` on the SP's directory object (when in scope) |

### Are these attributes pre-defined or assessment-defined?

**The *slot* is pre-defined. The *meaning* and the *contents* are
assessment-defined.**

- `extensionAttribute1` through `extensionAttribute15` are **standard,
  tenant-wide extensibility attributes** that exist on every Entra ID
  tenant out of the box. UIAO does not create them.
- What UIAO **does** define is the *contract*: "`extensionAttribute1`
  carries an OrgPath that must validate against the current codebook."
  That contract is declared canonically and asserted by the drift engine.
- The *values* themselves are populated during migration (Runbook
  [UIAO_156](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_156_Migration_Runbook.md))
  and maintained continuously by HR provisioning.

If a tenant is already using `extensionAttribute1` for something else, the
binding can be remapped — but only in the codebook frontmatter, not in
individual rules. That single rebinding propagates everywhere.

## Layer 3 — The authority lives in HR

Entra ID is the *system of record* for identity. The HR system is the
*system of truth* for organizational placement.

```
HR system (Workday / SAP HR / HRIS)
        │  authoritative org assignment
        ▼
Provisioning pipeline (HR-driven inbound provisioning, Entra Connect, or custom adapter)
        │  writes extensionAttribute1
        ▼
Entra ID user object
        │  read by dynamic-group / AU / CA / drift engines
        ▼
Downstream governance (Dynamic Groups · AUs · CA · Licensing · Drift)
```

Operating rule (from UIAO_151): **"HR system is the authoritative source —
IT never manually edits OrgPath values."** Any IT-originated write to
`extensionAttribute1` is itself a drift event under the provenance rules in
[UIAO_163 (Drift Engine)](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/UIAO_163_Drift_Detection_Engine.md).

## What about the `registry/` directory?

The repo's top-level [`registry/`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/tree/main/registry)
directory is a common point of confusion. It is **not** the OrgPath store.
It contains Governance OS adapter and pipeline manifests:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `manifest.json` · `adapter-manifest.json` | Adapter registration metadata |
| `modernization.json` | Pipeline sequencing for the modernization canon |
| `provenance.json` | Audit-trail schema |
| `boundaries.json` | GCC-Moderate scope definitions |

OrgPath data is **never** stored here.

## Quick reference

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| Where is the list of valid OrgPaths? | `src/uiao/canon/data/orgpath/codebook.yaml` |
| Where is a specific user's OrgPath? | `user.extensionAttribute1` in Entra ID |
| Who decides what value goes there? | The HR system, via provisioning |
| Are the Entra attributes UIAO-defined? | No — Microsoft provides them; UIAO claims them |
| Is there a database? | No — Git for vocabulary, Entra ID for values |
| What enforces the binding? | Drift engine (UIAO_163) against codebook schema (UIAO_158) |

## Related

- [OrgPath Codebook (UIAO_151)](codebook.qmd) — the canonical encoding scheme
- [OrgTree A-Z Suite](orgtree.qmd) — the full 28-artifact governance stack
- [Dynamic Groups](dynamic-groups.qmd) — how OrgPath drives group membership
- [Delegation Matrix](delegation.qmd) — how OrgPath drives AU scoping
